$schema: CCEM experiential learnings - patterns and anti-patterns discovered during migrations and project setup

learnings.title: Multi-project monorepo causes formation agent confusion
learnings.problem: GIMME project files (backend/, frontend/, supabase/) were tracked at the root of ~/Developer alongside unrelated projects (191919/, jeremiah_loves_bia/, idfwu2/). The git repo root was ~/Developer pointing to dnd.git. Formation agents spawned into worktrees inherited the entire multi-project tree, causing: (1) mypy/tsc commands failing due to wrong working directory assumptions, (2) git status showing 200+ unrelated untracked files, (3) CLAUDE.md needing caveats about repo root != project root.
learnings.solution: Extracted GIMME into standalone repo at ~/Developer/gimme/ with its own .git. Removed GIMME files from parent repo index (git rm --cached). Added gimme/ and project dirs to parent .gitignore. Updated CLAUDE.md and memory files to reference new root.
steps: cd gimme && git init && git add . && git commit
cd ~/Developer && git rm -r --cached backend/ frontend/ ... (all GIMME dirs)
Update memory files to reflect new structure
learnings.impact: Formation agents and worktrees now scope correctly to GIMME files only. No more ~/Developer catch-all confusion.
learnings.recommendation: When /upm plan or /formation deploy creates a new project, always verify the git root matches the project scope. If the git root contains multiple unrelated projects, extract to standalone repo BEFORE deploying formations.

learnings.title: CLAUDE.md must be updated when git root changes
learnings.problem: The CLAUDE.md file referenced '~/Developer' as the git root and all path examples assumed files were at that root level. After migrating to ~/Developer/gimme/, these references became incorrect and would confuse future agents.
learnings.solution: Updated CLAUDE.md Repository Layout section to reference ~/Developer/gimme/ as standalone repo. Updated memory files (reference_repo_structure.md) to note the migration date and new structure.
learnings.recommendation: After any git root migration, immediately update: (1) .claude/CLAUDE.md repo layout section, (2) memory files referencing paths, (3) formation configs if they embed absolute paths.

learnings.title: User-level vs project-level CLAUDE.md scoping
learnings.problem: Project config was in ~/.claude/projects/-Users-jeremiah-Developer-gimme/.claude/CLAUDE.md (user-level project scope) but the actual CLAUDE.md lived at ~/Developer/gimme/.claude/CLAUDE.md (repo-level). The user-level path encodes the working directory, so changing from ~/Developer to ~/Developer/gimme changes the project scope key.
learnings.solution: Ensured the repo-level .claude/CLAUDE.md is the source of truth. The user-level project directory (~/.claude/projects/-Users-jeremiah-Developer-gimme/) holds only memory files. No user-level CLAUDE.md override needed.
learnings.recommendation: Prefer repo-level .claude/CLAUDE.md for project instructions. Use user-level project directories only for memory persistence. The project scope key is derived from the working directory path, so it changes when the project moves.
